source_jobs.title: Senior Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



Job Summary: The Data Engineer plays a crucial role in building and maintaining the data infrastructure that supports our data strategy. This role focuses on contributing to the development of data pipelines, ensuring data quality, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver actionable insights. Working independently on assigned tasks and projects, the Data Engineer contributes to the overall effectiveness of the data department and supports the company's objective of leveraging data for continuous improvement and innovation.

Key Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

 1.  Participate in the design, development, and maintenance of data pipelines using ETL/ELT processes to ingest, transform, and load data from various sources into the data warehouse.

 2.  Implement and maintain data quality checks and validation processes to ensure data accuracy and reliability.

 3.  Collaborate with data scientists and analysts to understand their data requirements and provide solutions to meet their analytical needs.

 4.  Contribute to the development and maintenance of data models, schemas, and dictionaries to ensure data consistency and usability.

 5.  Monitor data pipeline performance and troubleshoot data-related issues in a timely manner.

 6.  Assist in the evaluation and implementation of new data technologies and tools.

 7.  Develop and maintain documentation for data pipelines, processes, and data models.

 8.  Contribute to the development of data governance policies and procedures.

 9.  Participate in code reviews and knowledge sharing sessions with other data engineers.

 10. Support the migration of existing data pipelines to more scalable and efficient solutions.

Qualifications & Desired Skills:

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field.

 - 4+ years of experience in data engineering or a similar role.

 - Proficiency in at least one programming language such as Python or Java.

 - Understanding of data warehousing concepts, modeling and methodologies.

 - Proficiency in SQL and experience with database management systems (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle).

 - Experience with optimizing complex queries.

 - Strong proficiency in using Git for version control and collaboration on codebase.

 - Experience with NoSQL databases (e.g., MongoDB).

 - Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP).

 - Experience with ETL/ELT tools such as Apache Airflow or similar.

 - Ability to work collaboratively with teams across data science, business intelligence, and software engineering to deliver high-quality data solutions.

 - Solid understanding of the importance of data validation and the basics of ensuring data quality (e.g., checking for duplicates, missing values, or incorrect data formats).

 - Basic knowledge of data modeling techniques.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

 - Excellent communication skills.




BENEFITS AND PERKS FOR PROPELUS EMPLOYEES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:

 - Awarded one of BuiltIn's 2025 Best Places to Work https://builtin.com/awards/colorado/2025/best-midsize-places-to-work and honored as a Silver Stevie® Award Winner in the 2025 Stevie Awards For Great Employers https://propelus.com/article/silver-stevie-award-2025-great-employers/.

 - Professional development allowance to help y

source_jobs.title: Director of Product (B2B)
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



We are looking for a (Sr.) Director of Product to lead the strategy and growth of our B2B product portfolio. This is a high-impact leadership role requiring deep expertise in Healthcare Product Leadership. You will be responsible for a portfolio of products that help healthcare organizations navigate the complexities of hiring, credentialing, and workforce compliance.

You will manage a team of Product Managers, driving revenue growth and market expansion while maintaining a culture of accountability and excellence. You will bridge the gap between complex regulatory mandates and seamless SaaS experiences, ensuring our solutions deliver measurable business value to our enterprise clients.



What You'll Do

 - Strategic Portfolio Leadership: Own the roadmap for the multi-product B2B healthcare suite. You will identify market expansion opportunities, manage pricing strategies, and drive product-led growth (PLG) initiatives.

 - Cultivating Product Ownership: Directly manage and coach a team of Product Managers. You will move the team away from a reactive, day-to-day tactical focus and foster a culture of deep product ownership and strategic accountability.

 - GTM & Cross-Functional Partnership: Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to execute cohesive Go-To-Market (GTM) strategies. You are responsible for revenue targets from a product-led perspective, identifying when to push into new markets and how to maximize NRR.

 - Regulatory Navigation: Translate the complex and shifting regulatory landscape into product opportunities. You will ensure our portfolio remains the gold standard for compliance while maintaining a fast pace of innovation.

 - Integrations & UX as a Value Driver: Act as the strategic lead for critical roadmap questions, such as integration strategy and redefining User Experience (UX) to drive business value.

 - Strategic Business Impact: Establish clear KPIs and reporting cadences to communicate ROI and business cases to the C-suite. You will be the primary advocate for the B2B portfolio's performance and future investment needs.



What You’ll Bring

 - B2B Healthcare Expertise: 7–10+ years of product management and/or leadership experience, specifically within Healthcare SaaS, Workforce Management, Credentialing, or HR Compliance.

 - At least 3 years of experience managing and developing a team of Product Managers, with a proven ability to level up a team's strategic thinking.

 - A track record of defining product visions and translating them into roadmaps that drive measurable revenue outcomes.

 - You are a self-starter who can take full ownership of a portfolio from Day 1, requiring limited oversight to execute complex, growth-oriented plans.

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication and storytelling skills. You can distill complex data into a clear vision that excites both internal teams and enterprise clients.

 - Deep understanding of product analytics, A/B testing, and conversion optimization to guide decision-making.

 - You treat your product portfolio like a business, with a deep understanding of unit economics, monetization models, and pricing strategy.

 - Experience with Agile/Scrum, a solid understanding of AI/ML applications, and proficiency in tools like Jira, Productboard, and Confluence




BENEFITS AND PERKS FOR PROPELUS EMPLOYEES INCLUDE BUT

source_jobs.title: Sales Executive
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



We are seeking a high-energy, new logo Sales Executive to drive revenue growth across our workforce compliance, employee health, and healthcare data intelligence suite. This is a pure hunter role, and you will be responsible for breaking into greenfield territories and securing new enterprise healthcare accounts.

As a trusted advisor, you'll build strong customer relationships, navigate increasingly complex sales scenarios, and guide customers through the enterprise buying journey by developing compelling value narratives, aligning cross-functional stakeholders, and creating scalable adoption strategies.

This role requires a self-motivated individual with a competitive spirit who embodies our brand's ethos and values and is eager to champion our solutions within the dynamic healthcare landscape. This position plays a critical role in shaping the future of healthcare technology while making a tangible impact on the industry's evolution.



What You'll Do

 - New Logo Acquisition: Own the full outbound lifecycle for large-scale healthcare enterprises. You are responsible for identifying, prospecting, and winning large "greenfield" accounts that span multiple states with a heavy focus in California.

 - Pipeline Management: Build and maintain a 3x qualified pipeline. You must be disciplined in your use of CRM to track every stage of the outbound funnel.

 - Top-of-Funnel Mastery: Execute a high-volume outbound motion independently. You are responsible for self-sourcing your own pipeline through targeted cold outreach and strategic networking within the healthcare space.

 - Strategic Stakeholder Engagement: Build consensus across complex healthcare organizations. You will identify operational pain points and connect our solutions to the strategic initiatives of the HR and Clinical C-Suite.

 - Complex Deal Navigation: Lead enterprise-level vendor selections and bid processes for $100K+ ARR opportunities, navigating the technical, legal, and clinical requirements unique to healthcare SaaS.



What You'll Bring

 - Proven success selling Workforce, Credentialing, or Healthcare Data solutions to large-scale multi-location healthcare enterprises with multiple stakeholders.

 - A multi-year track record of consistently exceeding $1M+ annual quotas selling complex SaaS technologies to large healthcare organizations.

 - Demonstrated success closing $100K+ ARR sales that require deep executive engagement and building consensus across multiple departments.A documented history of New Logo acquisition, with a focus on breaking into greenfield accounts rather than managing or expanding existing business.

 - You thrive in an environment where you are responsible for your own lead generation and are comfortable building a territory from zero.

 - The ability to identify business impacts and translate technical features into high-level strategic value for hospital leadership.

Qualifications & Desired Skills

 - 5+ years of B2B SaaS enterprise sales experience in the healthcare ecosystem, including experience selling complex software solutions to healthcare organizations, large health systems, background screeners, Credentialing Verification Organizations, or provider network workflow platforms.

 - Healthcare IT/SaaS expertise with a strong understanding of health system financial decision-making, hospital budgeting pro

source_jobs.title: Sr. Customer Success Manager - Government
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



The Senior Customer Success Manager (Government) is a strategic role designed for an expert navigator of the public sector. You will act as the primary partner for our most complex clients, including government regulatory boards, regulators, departments of health, state agencies, and associations.

Success is defined by how effectively our technology supports the mission-critical mandates of public safety and professional integrity. You will take end-to-end ownership of the customer lifecycle, align our platform with agency missions, drive measurable compliance outcomes, and ensure sustained value realization across your portfolio..



What You’ll Do

 - Strategic Relationship Management: Serve as the primary owner for a portfolio of government regulatory boards and state agencies, building trusted relationships with Executive Directors and state officials to ensure long-term stability.

 - Mission-Aligned Strategy: Develop success plans that align our technology with specific agency mandates, Department of Health regulations, and public-sector risk management priorities.

 - Regulatory Enablement: Lead customized training programs that empower staff within regulators and associations to fully utilize the platform for audit readiness and governance excellence.

 - Health & Usage Monitoring: Take end-to-end ownership of monitoring engagement metrics, translating data into "State of the Agency" insights that demonstrate sustained value and support long-term retention.

 - Complex Issue Triage: Act as the lead problem-solver for complex account hurdles, collaborating with internal product and government affairs teams to navigate the unique requirements of the government landscape.

 - Business Reviews: Conduct high-level strategic reviews to assess progress, identifying opportunities to deepen the partnership and improve compliance outcomes for regulators and boards.



What You’ll Bring

 - SaaS Government Expertise: 5+ years of B2B SaaS Customer Success or Account Management experience specifically managing government, regulatory, or public health accounts. You understand the red tape, the RFP cycles, and the unique stakeholders of the public sector.

 - Direct experience with healthcare compliance, state departments of health, or professional licensing is strongly preferred. You are comfortable speaking the language of regulators and state officials.

 - Exceptional ability to communicate with non-technical government leaders. You can distill complex software capabilities into a clear vision that aligns with public policy goals.

 - A self-sufficient professional who thrives in a remote environment. You are comfortable taking over a complex book of business and navigating multi-layered organizations with limited oversight.

 - A problem-solver who can identify a potential churn risk a year in advance and build the strategic rapport necessary to save the relationship.

 - Awarded one of BuiltIn's 2025 Best Places to Work https://builtin.com/awards/colorado/2025/best-midsize-places-to-work and honored as a Silver Stevie® Award Winner in the 2025 Stevie Awards For Great Employers https://propelus.com/article/silver-stevie-award-2025-great-employers/.

 - Professional development allowance to help you grow in the ways that mean the mo

source_jobs.title: Senior Performance Marketing Manager (B2C)
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



Job Summary

As the Senior Performance Marketing Manager, you will own the paid search, paid social, and search engine optimization strategy that drives subscriber acquisition and revenue growth for the Propelus CE Broker product line. You will be the in-house expert on B2C, subscription-based search marketing, responsible for translating individual healthcare professional intent into qualified subscription starts and long-term lifetime value. You will architect and execute full-funnel PPC and SEO programs, build the measurement framework that ties every dollar to subscription growth, and embed AI across the workflow—from keyword research and creative production to bid management, landing-page optimization, and reporting. Partnering closely with the Senior Director of Growth Marketing and, on a dotted-line basis, the Director of Growth Product Management, you will operate at the intersection of marketing and product, ensuring acquisition strategy and on-site experience compound together to grow CE Broker subscriptions.

Key Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

 - Own end-to-end B2C paid search and paid social strategy (Google Ads, Microsoft Ads, Performance Max, Demand Gen, Meta, TikTok, Reddit, YouTube) to drive new subscription acquisition, trial-to-paid conversion, and reactivation for the CE Broker product line.

 - Build and lead the organic search program for CE Broker—technical SEO, content SEO, on-page optimization, internal linking, and authority-building—targeting individual healthcare professionals searching for license renewal, CE tracking, and continuing education solutions.

 - Monitor cebroker.com http://cebroker.com web and data analytics using GA4, Google Search Console, and business intelligence tools (including Looker/Data Studio or other platforms) — tracking traffic volume, channel mix shifts, user behavior, and anomalies; surface insights on fluctuations in organic, direct, referral, and paid traffic to inform strategy and flag issues early.

 - Develop the subscription-economics measurement framework (CAC, payback period, LTV:CAC, ROAS by cohort, retention-adjusted ROAS) and use it to allocate budget across channels, campaigns, geographies, and license-board audiences.

 - Embed AI across the search workflow: deploy LLM-driven keyword and SERP research, AI-assisted ad copy and landing-page generation, automated bid and budget pacing, predictive audience modeling, and AI-powered reporting and anomaly detection.

 - Design and run a continuous experimentation roadmap—ad creative, bidding strategies, audiences, landing pages, pricing presentation, and checkout flows—using A/B and incrementality testing to compound subscription growth.

 - Optimize the subscriber funnel in partnership with the Director of Growth Product Management, influencing landing pages, pricing pages, sign-up flows, and on-site search experiences so that paid and organic traffic convert at best-in-class rates.

 - Develop and own the CE Broker SEO content strategy in partnership with Growth Marketing and Product Marketing—mapping search intent across license types, state boards, and renewal cycles to a high-velocity publishing roadmap.

 - Manage paid media budget and forecast subscription acquisition, delivering monthly and quarterly plans with channel mix, expected CAC, expected new subscribers, and d

source_jobs.title: Regulatory Partnerships Manager (or Director)
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



We are seeking a relationship-first professional who builds trust with state healthcare regulatory boards and agencies to support the growth and expansion of our Propelus solutions. This role is grounded in credibility—taking the time to understand stakeholder priorities and operational challenges, and mapping them to the CE Broker solution to deliver measurable value.

This is a consultative partnership role, not a traditional high-volume sales position. The successful candidate will earn trust within government environments, navigate multi-year decision cycles with patience, and maintain the discipline needed to move opportunities forward. You will serve as the primary point of contact for assigned state partners, guiding them on how to improve operational efficiency and strengthen compliance.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES & MEASURES OF SUCCESS

 - Execute Relationship Cadence: Maintain a consistent and proactive communication schedule with assigned state regulatory boards and agencies. Invest time to understand local missions and constraints. Success is measured by consistent engagement with key stakeholders and the progression of multi-year contract renewals and expansions.

 - Tactical Solution Mapping: Translate CE Broker’s capabilities into specific outcomes tied to agency efficiency and public protection. Deliver business cases and proposals that address the immediate needs of board staff and leadership. Success is measured by the ability to align our solution value with the agency’s specific operational goals.

 - Manage Opportunities Through the Lifecycle: Advance opportunities using a consultative approach. Create momentum by aligning our implementation timelines with agency deadlines and strategic plans. Success is reflected in steady pipeline progression and high conversion rates of assigned opportunities.

 - Regulatory Knowledge Application: Maintain an up-to-date understanding of state licensing frameworks and continuing education (CE) compliance for your assigned territories and professions.

 - Pipeline Coordination: Maintain a healthy and accurate pipeline by proactively surfacing new opportunities within existing or prospective accounts. Leverage industry trends to identify where Propelus can solve emerging compliance gaps.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with internal Product, Implementation, and Customer Success teams to ensure a seamless transition from contract signature to "go-live." Success is reflected in smooth hand-offs and positive partner feedback during the onboarding phase.

 - Operational Discipline: Maintain accurate visibility into opportunities via CRM (Salesforce). Bring structure to forecasting and follow-through to ensure no stakeholder request is left unanswered.


EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–5+ years of experience in public sector account management, healthcare, regulatory technology, or a related field.

 - Proven track record of managing relationships within state agencies or licensing boards, with an ability to build rapport at both the operational and leadership levels.

 - Project management mindset: Ability to coordinate complex RFPs and contract renewals, ensuring all technical and compliance requirements are met.

 - Patient persistence: Comfortable navigating the long, often non-linear decision cycles typical of government procurement.

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



Propelus is seeking a high-performing Customer Support Specialist to join our CE Broker team. CE Broker is the official continuing education tracking system for over 100 regulatory boards, ensuring healthcare professionals remain compliant and licensed. This is a high-volume, remote role designed for "power users" who thrive in a fast-paced SaaS environment. You will serve as the human connection in an AI-augmented support workflow, providing essential technical assistance to licensed professionals. If you are customer-centric, tech-adaptable, and enjoy the challenge of resolving complex user issues through live support, we want to hear from you.



What You'll Do

High-Volume Technical Execution

 - Multichannel Inbound Support: Provide expert assistance via live chat, email, and phone. Inbound phone support can account for up to 80–100% of your daily activity to meet the needs of the business.

 - Efficiency & Quality: Maintain a high performance standard, consistently resolving a minimum of 8 tickets per hour while maintaining top-tier customer satisfaction scores.

 - AI-Augmented Workflow: Work alongside our AI agents, who handle initial triage. You will step in to manage complex human interactions and ensure a complete customer journey.

Technical Troubleshooting & Feedback

 - Problem Solving: Go beyond the script to troubleshoot software bugs and navigation issues. You will be the "power user" of the CE Broker platform.

 - Account Education: Educate customers on our upgraded CE tracking account options, helping them understand the features available to them and guiding them toward the right plan for their needs.

 - Voice of the Customer: Proactively surface product feedback and recurring friction points to our internal teams to help improve the CE Broker user experience.

 - Process Improvement: Collaborate with the team to enhance our internal knowledge base and streamline support documentation.

What You’ll Bring

 - SaaS Support Experience: 2+ years in a contact center or technical support role, specifically within the SaaS industry.

 - Proficiency with Zendesk (or a similar CRM) is required. You must be comfortable navigating deep ticket histories and utilizing macros efficiently.

 - A positive mindset toward the evolution of AI in the workplace and a proven ability to learn new software platforms quickly.

 - Excellent verbal and written English. You can translate technical concepts into simple, friendly instructions for non-technical users. (Fluency in Spanish is a major plus!)

 - Associate’s degree or higher is preferred.

Remote Work Requirements

 - A quiet, dedicated, and professional home workspace.

 - High-Speed Internet: Minimum 25–50 Mbps download / 5–10 Mbps upload.

 - VOIP Stability: A stable connection capable of supporting consistent, high-quality phone calls throughout your entire shift.




BENEFITS AND PERKS FOR PROPELUS EMPLOYEES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:

 - Awarded one of BuiltIn's 2025 Best Places to Work https://builtin.com/awards/colorado/2025/best-midsize-places-to-work and honored as a Silver Stevie® Award Winner in the 2025 Stevie Awards For Great Employers https://propelus.com/article/silver-stevie-award-2025-great-employers/.

 - Professional development allowance to help you grow in the ways that mean the most to you.

 - Flexibility for balancing work w

source_jobs.title: Data Analytics lll
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



As an Data Analytics III, you will play a crucial role in transforming raw data into actionable insights to support data-driven decision-making across the company. You will work independently on assigned projects, collaborating with data scientists, product managers, and engineers to build, maintain, and improve our data infrastructure and analytics pipelines. Your work will contribute directly to the efficiency and effectiveness of our compliance management technology, enabling us to better serve our clients and drive innovation. You will contribute to the delivery of key datasets and reports, ensuring data quality and accessibility.

Key Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

 1.  Develop and maintain robust and scalable data pipelines to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into our data warehouse.

 2.  Design, build, and optimize data models to support analytical needs and reporting requirements.

 3.  Collaborate with data scientists to implement and deploy machine learning models and analytical solutions.

 4.  Monitor data quality and identify and resolve data integrity issues.

 5.  Contribute to the development of data documentation and metadata management practices.

 6.  Work with stakeholders to understand their data needs and translate them into technical requirements.

 7.  Assist in the evaluation and implementation of new data technologies and tools.

 8.  Participate in code reviews and contribute to the development of coding standards and best practices.

 9.  Independently manage assigned tasks and projects, ensuring timely delivery and quality results.

 10. Proactively identify opportunities to improve data processes and systems.

Qualifications & Desired Skills:

 1.  Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.

 2.  3+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or a similar role.

 3.  Proficiency in SQL and experience with relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL).

 4.  Familiarity with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Google Cloud, Azure) and related data warehousing services (e.g., Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery).

 5.  Familiarity with data testing frameworks.

 6.  Experience with ETL tools and techniques (e.g., Apache Airflow, dbt).

 7.  Solid understanding of data modeling principles and data warehouse architecture.

 8.  Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

 9.  Experience with at least one programming language (e.g., Python, Java).

 10. Good communication and collaboration skills.

 11. Proficiency in using Git for version control and collaboration on codebase.




BENEFITS AND PERKS FOR PROPELUS EMPLOYEES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:

 - Awarded one of BuiltIn's 2025 Best Places to Work https://builtin.com/awards/colorado/2025/best-midsize-places-to-work and honored as a Silver Stevie® Award Winner in the 2025 Stevie Awards For Great Employers https://propelus.com/article/silver-stevie-award-2025-great-employers/.

 - Professional development allowance to help you grow in the ways that mean the most to you.

 - Flexibility for balancing work with the rest of life and ample PTO, including paid time off for volunteering, your birthday, and becoming a new parent.

 - Check us out for yourself at our careers page https://propelus.com/careers/ or our Propelus culture

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer,
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



As a Senior Software Engineer, you'll be a hands-on technical leader who ships. Your main mission for the year ahead is to design, build, and launch our React Native companion app and get it into the App Store and Google Play. You'll work independently where it counts and alongside our web, API, and design teams to bring our compliance platform to mobile. You'll own features from end to end across a unified TypeScript stack, and you'll set the bar for quality, accessibility, and reliability along the way.



We have built a large-scale compliance platform for licensed professionals. It runs on a cloud-native monorepo with a unified TypeScript stack. We work in a regulated space, so ADA compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA) and solid engineering matter here in a concrete way: real people use this to keep their licenses and their jobs.


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN YOUR FIRST YEAR

 - Our React Native companion app is live in the App Store and Google Play, and licensed professionals are actually using it.

 - The mobile app shares logic and types with our web platform instead of forking into a second codebase.

 - The app holds the same accessibility and reliability bar as everything else we ship.

 - The engineers around you do better mobile and full-stack work because of the patterns and reviews you put in place.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the React Native companion app from architecture through app-store release, instrumentation, and the iteration that follows launch.

 - Work side by side with the web application team to build out new features for both web and mobile.

 - Ship full-stack features across accessible React UIs, type-safe APIs, and a GraphQL layer over several data stores, from first design to production.

 - Weigh architectural trade-offs and explain them clearly to both technical and non-technical people, so engineering decisions stay tied to business goals.

 - Stay accountable for what you ship. That includes instrumentation, handling incidents, and the analysis afterward.

 - Make the team better through careful code reviews, mentoring, and sharing what you know.

 - Improve how we deliver software: our testing frameworks, our CI/CD pipelines, and the tooling that gets in everyone's way when it's not right.


WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

 - You've built and shipped mobile apps to the App Store and Google Play (React Native strongly preferred), and you know what app review, release, and the messy weeks afterward actually take.

 - Deep hands-on full-stack experience with React, Node.js, TypeScript, and modern cloud-native development. You're still the person who writes the hard code.

 - Strong system-design instincts. You think about reliability, scalability, how things fail, and what a codebase feels like to maintain years later, and you build with that in mind.

 - Real production experience with AWS, modern CI/CD, observability, and running the systems you build.

 - Comfort tuning data access across relational, NoSQL, and search systems.

 - A track record of setting technical direction and mentoring engineers on a collaborative, asynchronous, distributed team.

 - Good judgment about when to pay down technical debt and when to keep shipping.

 - Experience building accessible web applications (WCAG 2.1 AA)



On seniority: the experience above usually adds up to something like 8 or more years buildi

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Analytics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



Job Summary: As an Analytics Engineer, you will play a crucial role in transforming raw data into actionable insights to support data-driven decision-making across the company. You will work independently on assigned projects, collaborating with data scientists, product managers, and engineers to build, maintain, and improve our data infrastructure and analytics pipelines. Your work will contribute directly to the efficiency and effectiveness of our compliance management technology, enabling us to better serve our clients and drive innovation. You will contribute to the delivery of key datasets and reports, ensuring data quality and accessibility.

Key Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

 1.  Develop and maintain robust and scalable data pipelines to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into our data warehouse.

 2.  Design, build, and optimize data models to support analytical needs and reporting requirements.

 3.  Collaborate with data scientists to implement and deploy machine learning models and analytical solutions.

 4.  Monitor data quality and identify and resolve data integrity issues.

 5.  Contribute to the development of data documentation and metadata management practices.

 6.  Work with stakeholders to understand their data needs and translate them into technical requirements.

 7.  Assist in the evaluation and implementation of new data technologies and tools.

 8.  Participate in code reviews and contribute to the development of coding standards and best practices.

 9.  Independently manage assigned tasks and projects, ensuring timely delivery and quality results.

 10. Proactively identify opportunities to improve data processes and systems.

Qualifications & Desired Skills:

 1.  Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.

 2.  4+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or a similar role.

 3.  Proficiency in SQL and experience with relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL).

 4.  Familiarity with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Google Cloud, Azure) and related data warehousing services (e.g., Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery).

 5.  Familiarity with data testing frameworks.

 6.  Experience with ETL tools and techniques (e.g., Apache Airflow, dbt).

 7.  Solid understanding of data modeling principles and data warehouse architecture.

 8.  Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

 9.  Experience with at least one programming language (e.g., Python, Java).

 10. Good communication and collaboration skills.

 11. Proficiency in using Git for version control and collaboration on codebase.




BENEFITS AND PERKS FOR PROPELUS EMPLOYEES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:

 - Awarded one of BuiltIn's 2025 Best Places to Work https://builtin.com/awards/colorado/2025/best-midsize-places-to-work and honored as a Silver Stevie® Award Winner in the 2025 Stevie Awards For Great Employers https://propelus.com/article/silver-stevie-award-2025-great-employers/.

 - Professional development allowance to help you grow in the ways that mean the most to you.

 - Flexibility for balancing work with the rest of life and ample PTO, including paid time off for volunteering, your birthday, and becoming a new parent.

 - Check us out for yourself at our careers page https://propelus.com/careers/ or our Prop

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager - Immuware
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



As a Customer Success Manager, you will be responsible for ensuring the success and satisfaction of a portfolio of our customers. You will proactively engage with customers, build strong relationships, and guide them through the onboarding process and beyond. You will identify opportunities for product adoption and expansion, contributing to customer retention and revenue growth. Working independently, you'll be responsible for managing your own accounts and contributing to team initiatives.

 1.  Manage a portfolio of assigned customers, serving as their primary point of contact and advocate.

 2.  Build and maintain strong, long-lasting relationships with key stakeholders across customer organizations.

 3.  Partner with Professional Services to onboard new customers and foster a smooth transition to ensure long-term success.

 4.  Collaborate with customers to create and execute shared success plans, aligning with their business goals to drive continuous value.

 5.  Proactively monitor customer health and leverage the CSP system to stay data-driven in our approach, utilizing playbooks to address potential risks, track customer interactions, and ensure timely follow-up.

 6.  Partner with internal teams, including Support and Product, to resolve customer challenges and ensure their needs are met.

 7.  Conduct regular touch points and business reviews to assess progress, discuss goals, and uncover opportunities for improvement.

 8.  Provide ongoing support and guidance to customers on product features, best practices, and strategies for maximizing value.

 9.  Identify upsell, cross-sell and expansion opportunities within existing accounts, fostering stronger relationships and driving additional value.

 5.  Proven ability to build and maintain strong customer relationships.

 6.  Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

 7.  Proficiency in using CRM/CSP software (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot).

 8.  Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

 9.  Experience with compliance management software is a plus.

 10. Certification in Customer Success Management is an advantage.

source_jobs.title: UX Designer Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Propelus® simplifies workforce compliance management across healthcare. Our innovative technology and strategic partnerships empower millions of professionals, their employers, and regulators to work together, creating a connected and efficient healthcare ecosystem.

As a trusted leader for over 20 years, Propelus has focused on bringing more good into the lives of the people and organizations serving healthcare. We deliver seamless compliance solutions to millions. We leverage market-leading technology and essential data to simplify complex operations, reduce risk, and champion a safer, healthier, and happier workforce.



Overview | We are looking for a UX Engineer who designs and ships. This is a design-leaning hybrid role: you own the user experience end to end, from research and interaction design through high-fidelity, production-ready front-end. You will spend most of your time in design and UX, and you will use Claude Design or similar AI tools to turn your designs into working code that engineers can build on without a traditional handoff.

This role sits on the Product team and focuses on our CE Broker professional application and marketplace and our Evercheck HR compliance tools. You will partner closely with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to create intuitive, accessible interfaces that drive real business outcomes. As a Level III individual contributor, you are expected to lead design-engineering discussions and raise the quality bar across the work you own.

The work happens in regulated healthcare contexts, so accessibility, clarity, and trust are not optional. The best candidate treats them as core craft.



Core Responsibilities | This role includes but is not limited to:

 - Lead the design and redesign of full application experiences across CE Broker from early research through shipped UI.

 - Conduct and synthesize user research with product managers to understand user needs, identify pain points, and translate insights into design decisions.

 - Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity mockups for web and mobile, and validate them with real users before build.

 - Build production-quality front-end code for your designs using React and Tailwind CSS, with a focus on reliability, accessibility, and performance.

 - Use Claude Design to move from design to working code quickly, and review that output for quality, edge cases, and maintainability before it reaches engineering.

 - Own and extend our design system: components, tokens, patterns, and documentation that keep the experience consistent across products.

 - Lead design-engineering discussions and contribute to decisions about interaction patterns, component architecture, and design system direction.

 - Ensure ADA and accessibility compliance and front-end best practices across everything you ship.

 - Stay current on UX, front-end, and AI tooling trends, and bring back what is worth adopting.



Qualifications & Expertise |

 - 4 to 7 years of experience as a UX/product designer with a strong portfolio of shipped products.

 - Demonstrated ability to lead full application design and redesign projects end to end.

 - Proficiency with modern design and prototyping tools. Experience with Figma is a plus.

 - Front-end development skills using React and Tailwind CSS, with the ability to produce production-ready code.

 - Fluency with AI-assisted design and coding tools, including Claude Design, and sound judgment about when to rely on AI output and when to refine it by hand. This is a core expectation of the role, not a nice-to-have.

 - Experience building and maintaining design systems.

 - A strong grasp of user-centered design, product thinking, and how design connects to business strategy.

 - Understanding of ADA compliance and accessibility standards (WCAG).

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to articulate design and technical decisions clearly to designers, engineers, and product partners.

notes: Propelus is a healthcare compliance management company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
